summary: Checked 18 claims, found 3 mirages (1 critical, 2 major)

findings.claim: tech-spec says: @MockBean for ReactiveMongoClient to prevent real DB connections in integration tests (Decision 6, Task 5, Testing Strategy)
findings.reality: There is no class named ReactiveMongoClient in Spring Data MongoDB or the MongoDB driver. The actual class is com.mongodb.reactivestreams.client.MongoClient (from the MongoDB Reactive Streams driver). Spring Data MongoDB does not expose a class named ReactiveMongoClient. Using @MockBean(ReactiveMongoClient.class) will fail to compile or will not match the auto-configured bean, making the test strategy non-functional.
findings.source: tech-spec.md, Decision 6 (@MockBean for DB auto-config), Testing Strategy (Integration tests), Task 5 description
findings.fix: Replace ReactiveMongoClient with com.mongodb.reactivestreams.client.MongoClient in all @MockBean usages. The correct pattern is: @MockBean com.mongodb.reactivestreams.client.MongoClient mongoClient. Alternatively mock ReactiveMongoTemplate or use @DataMongoTest slice.

findings.claim: tech-spec says: Spring Boot 3.4.x is 'the stable LTS line' (Decision 1, Solution section)
findings.reality: Spring Boot 3.4.x reached End of Life on December 31, 2025 and is no longer receiving security updates or patches. As of May 2026, the current actively-supported 3.x line is Spring Boot 3.5.x (support ends June 30, 2026). The tech-spec rationale ('Boot 3.4.x is the stable LTS line') is factually incorrect — 3.4.x is EOL. The correct alternative within the 3.x family would be 3.5.x. Note: the decision to stay on 3.x over 4.x is reasonable, but the version chosen within 3.x is wrong.
findings.source: tech-spec.md, Decision 1 (Spring Boot 3.4.x over 4.0.x), Solution section
findings.fix: Change the target version from Spring Boot 3.4.x to Spring Boot 3.5.x (latest: 3.5.13 as of March 2026). Update all references: build.gradle Spring Boot plugin version, tech-spec Solution section, Decision 1 rationale, User-Spec Deviations section. Spring Boot 3.5.x uses the same Spring Framework 6.x / Spring Security 6.x stack, so no API changes are required.

findings.claim: tech-spec says: compatibilityDate is a 'required' field in Nuxt 4 nuxt.config.ts (Decision 2, Task 3)
findings.reality: compatibilityDate is strongly recommended but not strictly required in Nuxt 4. Nuxt 4 enables all v4 features by default and does not throw an error if compatibilityDate is absent. The field controls preset behavior in Nitro and other modules but its absence does not prevent the project from building or running. Nuxt CLI (nuxi init) generates it by default, making it a de-facto standard but not a hard requirement.
findings.source: tech-spec.md, Decision 2 (Nuxt 4 — 'Requires compatibilityDate field'), Task 3 description
findings.fix: Change 'required' to 'strongly recommended' in Decision 2 and Task 3 description. The field should still be included in nuxt.config.ts (it is generated by nuxi init and controls preset behavior), but the wording should reflect that its absence does not break the build.

verified_claims: @EnableWebFluxSecurity annotation exists and is correct for WebFlux in Spring Security 6
@Configuration required alongside @EnableWebFluxSecurity (not included in the annotation since Spring Security 6)
SecurityWebFilterChain bean via ServerHttpSecurity is the correct WebFlux security pattern
anyExchange().authenticated() is valid in Spring Security 6 WebFlux
ReactiveRedisConnectionFactory class exists in Spring Data Redis
@SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = RANDOM_PORT) + @AutoConfigureWebTestClient is correct WebFlux integration test pattern
developmentOnly Gradle configuration name is correct — Spring Boot Gradle plugin auto-creates it since 2.3
org.gradle.toolchains.foojay-resolver-convention is the correct Foojay plugin ID
Foojay resolver plugin version 1.0.0 is current as of 2026
mongo:8.0 Docker Hub image tag exists
redis:7.4 Docker Hub image tag exists
Node.js 24 is active LTS as of May 2026 (LTS through April 2028)
Nuxt 4.0 stable release date was July 15, 2025
Nuxt 4.4.x exists — 4.4 released March 2026, 4.4.3 released April 2026
Spring Boot 3.4.x uses Spring Framework 6.x and Spring Security 6.x (correct stack)
